name: healthcare_near_miss_event_generates_oakline_auto_ben
premise: A Healthcare clinical command center is in a fast-moving incident involving healthcare near miss event generates oakline auto ben. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is within 20 minutes. Every delay increases patient safety, triage integrity, and privacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as autonomy vs beneficence under pressure. A live dashboard is public; inconsistencies will trigger immediate investigation. Constraints: Make the intervention reversible and time-boxed with named accountability. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Luc Haddad
agents.identity: I am Luc Haddad, accountable for a defensible call in Healthcare when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays beneficence for autonomy.

agents.name: Noah Santos
agents.identity: I am Noah Santos, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Healthcare.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so beneficence isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of autonomy.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect beneficence.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to beneficence to secure autonomy under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is within 20 minutes. Evidence is partial. Luc Haddad must decide under autonomy vs beneficence. What does Luc Haddad do?
